title: DCECE Bihar Polytechnic 2026 – What Is It?

detail.title: DCECE Bihar Polytechnic 2026 – What Is It?
detail.short_info: The DCECE Bihar Polytechnic Online Application Form 2026 is now live on the BCECEB portal. Check eligibility, documents, step‑by‑step filling guide, official links, and FAQs.

sections.heading: DCECE Bihar Polytechnic 2026 – What Is It?

content.text: The DCECE (Diploma Certificate Entrance Competitive Examination) is conducted by the Bihar Combined Entrance Competitive Examination Board (BCECEB) for admission to polytechnic diploma courses in Bihar. The DCECE Bihar Polytechnic Online Application Form 2026 must be filled on the official BCECEB portal for PE, PM, and PMM streams.

This entrance exam is the main gateway for students who have passed Class 10th / 12th (as per stream) and want to pursue technical or paramedical diplomas in government and private polytechnics in Bihar.

sections.heading: DCECE 2026 Important Dates (Overview Table)

rows: As per official notice on BCECEB website ​
To be announced; check official notice / prospectus

sections.heading: Who Can Apply? DCECE Eligibility 2026
For Diploma Engineering (PE)

items: Passed Class 10th with minimum 35% marks (varies slightly by category).
Studied Science, Mathematics, and English (or equivalent) in Class 10th.
Appearing candidates may also apply, but final admission depends on passing Class 10th.

sections.heading: For Para Medical (PM) and Pharmacy (PMM)

items: Passed Class 12th with Science (Physics, Chemistry, Biology) from a recognized board.
Minimum percentage criteria and age limits as per the official notification and prospectus.
Domicile rules may apply for some seats; check the DCECE 2026 Prospectus PDF for exact details.

content.text: Use a simple eligibility table in the article layout showing differences between PE, PM, and PMM (marks, subjects, domicile rules).

sections.heading: Documents Required Before Filling the Form

content.text: Before starting the DCECE Bihar Polytechnic Online Application Form 2026, keep these ready:

items: Active mobile number and email ID (for OTP and future communication).
Aadhaar card or any valid ID proof.
Class 10th and/or 12th mark sheets (photocopies + scanned copies).
Domicile certificate (if claiming domicile‑based reservation).
Recent passport‑size photograph (e.g., 200×200 or 300×300 px, usually < 100 KB).
Clear scanned signature (as per specifications).

content.text: Having these documents scanned and properly named will speed up the online filling process and reduce errors.

sections.heading: How to Fill DCECE Bihar Polytechnic Online Application Form 2026
Step 1 – Visit the Official BCECEB Website

content.text: Go to the official website: Click Here
Look for the DCECE 2026 / Bihar Polytechnic / Online Application section on the homepage or notice board. Click on the link for the DCECE Bihar Polytechnic Online Application Form 2026.

sections.heading: Step 2 – Register for a New Account

items: Click on the “New Registration” or “Apply Online” option.
Enter basic details such as: Name (as per Class 10/12 mark sheet) Date of birth Gender Category (General, OBC, SC, ST, etc.) Domicile (if applicable) Mobile number and email ID
Name (as per Class 10/12 mark sheet)
Date of birth
Gender
Category (General, OBC, SC, ST, etc.)
Domicile (if applicable)
Mobile number and email ID
Submit the form to generate a registration/login ID and password.
Note down these credentials; you will need them to log in later.

sections.heading: Step 3 – Login and Fill the Application

items: Return to the portal and click “Login / Existing User”.
Enter your Registration ID (or mobile/mail) and Password.

Fill the main application form fields carefully: Personal details (name, address, parents’ details). Academic details (board, school, year of passing, marks). Category and reservation details (if any). Course choice (PE / PM / PMM) and preferred branches/colleges.
Personal details (name, address, parents’ details).
Academic details (board, school, year of passing, marks).
Category and reservation details (if any).
Course choice (PE / PM / PMM) and preferred branches/colleges.

content.text: Important:

items: Double‑check that your name, date of birth, and category exactly match your mark sheets and ID proof.
Any mismatch can cause rejection or trouble during counselling.

sections.heading: Step 4 – Upload Photo and Signature

items: Go to the “Upload Documents” section.
Upload: Recent passport‑size photograph (clear, front‑facing, no filters). Clear scanned signature on white background.
Recent passport‑size photograph (clear, front‑facing, no filters).
Clear scanned signature on white background.
Follow the size and format instructions: Image size usually under 100 KB and in JPG/JPEG/PNG format. Resolution often around 200×200 or 300×300 pixels.
Image size usually under 100 KB and in JPG/JPEG/PNG format.
Resolution often around 200×200 or 300×300 pixels.

content.text: Corrupted or oversized files may get rejected, so use a simple image‑editing tool or online compressor if needed.

sections.heading: Step 5 – Verify and Preview the Form

items: Before paying the fee, click on “Preview Form” or “View”.
Check: All personal and academic details. Course and category selection. Uploaded photo and signature.
All personal and academic details.
Course and category selection.
Uploaded photo and signature.
If any error is found, use the “Edit” option (if available in the active window).
If the correction window has already closed, contact BCECEB support or check the official notice for instructions.

sections.heading: Step 6 – Pay the Application Fee

items: After successful verification, proceed to the “Payment” section.
Pay via: Debit card Credit card Net banking UPI or other online payment options listed on the portal
Debit card
Credit card
Net banking
UPI or other online payment options listed on the portal

content.text: Common fee structure (may vary slightly by category and year):

items: General / OBC candidates: Around ₹1000 (check notification PDF for exact amount).
SC / ST candidates: Often reduced (around ₹500; verify in prospectus).

content.text: Once payment is successful, you should receive:

items: A confirmation message.
A payment receipt or transaction ID.
A filled application PDF (auto‑generated).

sections.heading: Step 7 – Download and Print the Application

items: After successful payment, download the DCECE 2026 Application Form PDF.
Print 2–3 copies for your records.
Keep the printout along with: Mark sheets Aadhaar card Fee receipt Any other documents mentioned in the prospectus
Mark sheets
Aadhaar card
Fee receipt
Any other documents mentioned in the prospectus

content.text: You will need these documents during counselling, admission, and verification at the polytechnic college.

sections.heading: Official Links for DCECE Bihar Polytechnic 2026

content.text: Use these direct links in your article (hyperlinked as buttons or text links):

items: DCECE Bihar Polytechnic 2026 Notification PDF: Click Here
DCECE 2026 Prospectus PDF (Download): Click Here
DCECE Bihar Polytechnic Online Application Form 2026 (Apply Link): Click Here → Look for DCECE / Bihar Polytechnic section
BCECEB Official Website: Click Here

content.text: Mention that candidates should never apply through third‑party websites and use only the official BCECEB portal.

sections.heading: Common Mistakes to Avoid

items: Entering wrong category or caste details (can lead to rejection or reservation issues).
Mismatch in name spelling between the application and mark sheet.
Uploading blurry or oversized photos/signatures.
Forgetting to pay the fee properly or missing the payment confirmation.
Missing the correction window for editing details.

content.text: Advising students to complete the form in the first week after opening (e.g., between 19–25 March 2026) helps avoid last‑minute server issues and internet problems.
